mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 7.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and deceleration in consumer price inflation indicates robust demand growth without inflationary pressure, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle phases, yielding negative 5-day returns when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor market strength and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain flat or negative despite payroll growth exceeding 150,000 per month for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2% over 60 days following CPIAUCSL threshold breaches, indicating inflation tolerance or growth-driven pricing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real gross domestic product confirms broad-based economic recovery, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns when HOUST growth outpaces GDPC1 growth by more than 1.5 percentage points, indicating sectoral imbalance.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly contractions in industrial production signal supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances following two consecutive INDPRO declines, indicating market decoupling from industrial data.
